## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Infrastructure work with one design disposition (self-update watcher off). Mostly shell-script modification to the existing deploy tabtarget plus a one-line disable in `main()`. Low ambiguity.
  
  ## Problem
  
  Ann has no way to run clipbuddy today. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` exists but (a) doesn't emit forwarding instructions Brad can relay to Ann, and (b) depends on a self-update watcher that's premature complexity — the "seamless hot-swap during active session" problem isn't one Ann has at this stage.
  
  ## Solution
  
  Treat Ann's workflow as manual quit + relaunch. The deploy tabtarget emits a formatted instruction block Brad copy-pastes to Ann after each deploy. The self-update watcher goes dormant — source stays intact for trivial revert if we ever want it back.
  
  ## Deliverables
  
  - `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`: after successful build + scp, emit a formatted block bracketed by `=== Forward to Ann ===` and `=== End ===` lines (easy visual selection for copy-paste). Instructions cover quit-if-running, launch Apcap.app from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` (Finder double-click or Terminal `open` command).
  - `apcap_main.rs::main()`: comment out `apcru_start_watcher()` call with a brief inline note explaining the disposition ("disabled for prototype — manual quit + relaunch via Shared/apcua/Apcap.app is the current deploy workflow").
  - Paddock note: self-update watcher dormant pending real need; Ann's deploy ceremony is manual.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Keep `apcru_update.rs` source intact — revert is a one-line uncomment.
  - Deploy tabtarget remains a single invocation — no additional manual steps for Brad.
  - Instruction block is the sole source of truth for Ann's ceremony (no paddock copy, no separate doc).
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` runs cleanly against Ann's machine (scp succeeds).
  - Printed instruction block is copy-paste-ready.
  - Ann launches the deployed app via the printed instructions (Brad verifies in person or via screen-share).
  - `~/apcjd/` is created on Ann's machine on first focus event (triggered during verification).
  - `apcap.log` on Ann's machine does NOT contain `"watching /Users/Shared/apcua for updates"` — confirming the watcher is dormant.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Onboarding documentation beyond the printed block.
  - Dock icon / LaunchPad integration for Ann.
  - Removing `apcru_update.rs` (disable-only; removal is a later cleanup concern).
  - ssh error handling beyond what scp produces.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
25	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
26	- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
27	- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
28	- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
29	- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
30	- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
31	- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
32	- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.
33	
34	## Authoritative Documents
35	
36	| Document | Owns | Path |

# jjezs_paddock ₣A9

## Character

Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.

## Problem

Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.

## Solution

Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation

## Key Design Decisions

- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.

## Authoritative Documents

| Document | Owns | Path |
|----------|------|------|
| Application Spec (APCAS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCAS-Specification.md` |
| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAS

## Character

Infrastructure work with one design disposition (self-update watcher off). Mostly shell-script modification to the existing deploy tabtarget plus a one-line disable in `main()`. Low ambiguity.

## Problem

Ann has no way to run clipbuddy today. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` exists but (a) doesn't emit forwarding instructions Brad can relay to Ann, and (b) depends on a self-update watcher that's premature complexity — the "seamless hot-swap during active session" problem isn't one Ann has at this stage.

## Solution

Treat Ann's workflow as manual quit + relaunch. The deploy tabtarget emits a formatted instruction block Brad copy-pastes to Ann after each deploy. The self-update watcher goes dormant — source stays intact for trivial revert if we ever want it back.

## Deliverables

- `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`: after successful build + scp, emit a formatted block bracketed by `=== Forward to Ann ===` and `=== End ===` lines (easy visual selection for copy-paste). Instructions cover quit-if-running, launch Apcap.app from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` (Finder double-click or Terminal `open` command).
- `apcap_main.rs::main()`: comment out `apcru_start_watcher()` call with a brief inline note explaining the disposition ("disabled for prototype — manual quit + relaunch via Shared/apcua/Apcap.app is the current deploy workflow").
- Paddock note: self-update watcher dormant pending real need; Ann's deploy ceremony is manual.

## Constraints

- Keep `apcru_update.rs` source intact — revert is a one-line uncomment.
- Deploy tabtarget remains a single invocation — no additional manual steps for Brad.
- Instruction block is the sole source of truth for Ann's ceremony (no paddock copy, no separate doc).

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` runs cleanly against Ann's machine (scp succeeds).
- Printed instruction block is copy-paste-ready.
- Ann launches the deployed app via the printed instructions (Brad verifies in person or via screen-share).
- `~/apcjd/` is created on Ann's machine on first focus event (triggered during verification).
- `apcap.log` on Ann's machine does NOT contain `"watching /Users/Shared/apcua for updates"` — confirming the watcher is dormant.

## Out of Scope

- Onboarding documentation beyond the printed block.
- Dock icon / LaunchPad integration for Ann.
- Removing `apcru_update.rs` (disable-only; removal is a later cleanup concern).
- ssh error handling beyond what scp produces.

    ## Character
    
    Mechanical application of settled decisions. The substantive design work happened in the session that produced this pace — every decision below is a write-down, not an open question. Sonnet-scale: judgment calls are narrow (prefix alignment, linked-term phrasing, quoin placement), but the decisions themselves need no re-deliberation.
    
    ## Context
    
    The Stanford spike (₢A9AAQ) exposed structural incoherence in APCS0. A design conversation reshaped the concept model along these lines:
    
    - **Byte ranges are implementation details, not vocabulary.** The concept model talks about regions of normalized text; byte offsets live in code only.
    - **Algorithms run in parallel on the same input**, not serially refining each other's output. Each discerner is a pure function of `apcs_normalized_text`.
    - **Severity vocabulary (RED/YELLOW/PASS) is dropped.** It conflated epistemic confidence, default disposition, and display affordance — three distinct things collapsed under one label.
    - **No generic `apcs_algorithm` or `apcs_reliability_profile`.** Discerners are named intimately; reconciliation rules reference them by name. Generic abstractions were premature and invited non-load-bearing complexity.
    - **Four concrete discerners**, each with its own registry/parameter quoin and its own finding-output quoin, shaped in parallel but intimately named (no common supertype).
    - **`apcs_reconciliation` is named** as the fusion procedure but its rules are deferred to the sibling pace `apcs-combining-algorithm`.
    
    ## Scope
    
    Apply these decisions to `Tools/apck/APCS0-SpecTop.adoc`. No code changes. No decisions re-opened.
    
    ### Quoins to install
    
    **New in the mapping section:**
    
    - `apcs_normalization` — one-time light cleanup procedure producing the canonical input
    - `apcs_normalized_text` — the single input every discerner consumes
    - `apcs_regex_scan` — the discerner (parameterized by `apcs_regex_table`, which remains)
    - `apcs_label_scan` — the discerner (parameterized by `apcs_label_registry`, which remains)
    - `apcs_dictionary_scan` — the discerner (parameterized by dictionaries + whitelists, which remain)
    - `apcs_stanford_ner` — the discerner (parameterized by the frozen ONNX model + its flat taxonomy)
    - `apcs_regex_finding`, `apcs_label_finding`, `apcs_dictionary_finding`, `apcs_stanford_finding` — per-discerner output quoins, sibling-shaped, intimately named
    - `apcs_reconciliation` — stub with pointer to the deferred section
    - `apcs_unified_classification` — the reconciliation output consumed by anonymization
    
    ### Quoins to remove
    
    - `apcs_severity`, `apcs_red`, `apcs_yellow`, `apcs_pass` — severity gestalt is abandoned
    - `apcs_merge` — reconciliation replaces merge and is deferred
    - `apcs_finding` as a generic type — replaced by four intimately-named per-discerner outputs
    
    ### Quoins to reshape
    
    - The "Pipeline Steps" section — no longer a linear sequence. Becomes three sections: `== Discerners` (parallel, four subsections), `== Reconciliation [DEFERRED]`, `== Anonymization`
    - `apcs_anonymized_text` — now consumes `apcs_unified_classification`, not the old merged-findings list
    - `apcs_assay` family — the diagnostic projection now renders per-discerner and per-unified views. Lift to a family with a variant dimension, or split as siblings (`apcs_discerner_assay`, `apcs_unified_assay`) — editor choice at write time
    - Dictionary scan contradiction — fix the RED-vs-YELLOW logic ambiguity in the prose to match actual code behavior (blacklist-only vs. blacklist+whitelist). Internal-disposition asymmetry flagged as deferred
    
    ### Deferred in this pace (explicit stubs)
    
    - `== Reconciliation` section: stub header with `[DEFERRED — see ₢A9AAS apcs-combining-algorithm]` flag
    - Role/disposition terminology (TEXT/PHI/DECIDE vs cover/surface vs other) marked deferred
    - Dictionary-finding internal-disposition schema marked deferred
    - Clinician-toggle composition marked deferred
    
    ### Non-goals
    
    - No reconciliation rules
    - No new disposition terminology
    - No code changes to `apcre_engine.rs` or any Rust file
    - No changes to APCAS, APCPS, or `apck-claude-context.md`
    
    ## Exit Criteria
    
    - `APCS0-SpecTop.adoc` renders via AsciiDoc without errors
    - Link validation clean — every `{apcs_*}` resolves to an anchor; every anchor has at least one attribute reference
    - Every removed quoin is either replaced in vocabulary or the removal is explained in the spec
    - Every deferred stub is clearly marked and points to ₢A9AAS
    - A fresh reader can understand the architectural shape (parallel discerners → deferred reconciliation → anonymization) from the document alone
    - The dictionary-scan internal contradiction is resolved in prose

73	
74	== Overview
75	
76	This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
77	It specifies the concrete entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the steps it performs.
78	
79	The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
80	The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
81	This document owns the detection algorithm's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.
82	
83	
84	== Input
85	
86	[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
87	{apcs_clipboard_content}::
88	Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
89	May be HTML or plain text.
90	Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.
91	
92	[[apcs_epic_html]]
93	{apcs_epic_html}::
94	HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
95	Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
96	The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.
97	
98	[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
99	{apcs_clinical_gate}::
100	A heuristic that decides whether
101	{apcs_clipboard_content}
102	is clinical.
103	Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
104	Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
105	Content that fails the
106	{apcs_clinical_gate}
107	is not processed; the triage view clears.
108	
109	
110	== Parsed Structure
111	
112	[[apcs_document]]
113	{apcs_document}::
114	The result of parsing
115	{apcs_epic_html}.
116	Contains a sequence of typed
117	{apcs_span_s}
118	and the derived
119	{apcs_plain_text}.
120	
121	[[apcs_plain_text]]
122	{apcs_plain_text}::
123	A single string derived from the
124	{apcs_document}
125	by stripping HTML markup, normalizing whitespace, and concatenating span contributions with structural line breaks.
126	All detection scans operate against
127	{apcs_plain_text}.
128	Offsets in
129	{apcs_finding_s}
130	are byte positions within this string.
131	
132	[[apcs_span]]
133	{apcs_span}::
134	A typed region within the
135	{apcs_document}.
136	Each
137	{apcs_span}
138	has a kind, an optional label, text content, and a byte offset into
139	{apcs_plain_text}.
140	Three kinds:
141	{apcs_labeled_field},
142	{apcs_section_header},
143	{apcs_narrative}.
144	
145	[[apcs_labeled_field]]
146	{apcs_labeled_field}::
147	A
148	{apcs_span}
149	produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
150	The label is the bold text before the colon.
151	The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).
152	
153	[[apcs_section_header]]
154	{apcs_section_header}::
155	A
156	{apcs_span}
157	produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
158	Contributes the header text to
159	{apcs_plain_text}
160	but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
161	{apcs_span_s}.
162	
163	[[apcs_narrative]]
164	{apcs_narrative}::
165	A
166	{apcs_span}
167	containing freetext not attached to a label.
168	Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
169	PHI in
170	{apcs_narrative}
171	{apcs_span_s}
172	lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by the
173	{apcs_regex_scan}
174	or
175	{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
176	
177	
178	== Detection Resources
179	
180	=== Registries
181	
182	[[apcs_label_registry]]
183	{apcs_label_registry}::
184	The enumerated set of
185	{apcs_labeled_field}
186	labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
187	{apcs_label_scan}.
188	Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
189	{apcs_phi_category}
190	assigned to extracted words.
191	+
192	[cols="2,1"]
193	|===
194	| Label | Category
195	
196	| Patient:           | NAME
197	| Attending:         | PROVIDER
198	| Provider:          | PROVIDER
199	| Referring:         | PROVIDER
200	| Facility:          | FACILITY
201	| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
202	| Emergency Contact  | NAME
203	| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
204	|===
205	
206	[[apcs_regex_table]]
207	{apcs_regex_table}::
208	The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
209	{apcs_regex_scan}.
210	Each entry specifies a pattern, a
211	{apcs_phi_category},
212	and a capture group index.
213	All matches produce
214	{apcs_red}
215	{apcs_finding_s}.
216	See APCPS for the full pattern table.
217	
218	=== Dictionaries
219	
220	[[apcs_blacklist]]
221	{apcs_blacklist}::
222	A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
223	{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
224	Three named
225	{apcs_blacklist_s}
226	are combined into a single lookup automaton:
227	
228	[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
229	{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
230	US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
231	Source: public domain.
232	
233	[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
234	{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
235	SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
236	Source: public domain.
237	
238	[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
239	{apcs_city_dictionary}::
240	US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
241	Source: public domain.
242	
243	[[apcs_whitelist]]
244	{apcs_whitelist}::
245	A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
246	Membership in a
247	{apcs_whitelist}
248	does not prevent a
249	{apcs_finding} —
250	a word in both a
251	{apcs_blacklist}
252	and a
253	{apcs_whitelist}
254	is classified
255	{apcs_yellow}.
256	Two named
257	{apcs_whitelist_s}:
258	
259	[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
260	{apcs_english_whitelist}::
261	Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
262	Source: public domain.
263	
264	[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
265	{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
266	Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
267	Source: original curation.
268	
269	
270	== Finding Anatomy
271	
272	[[apcs_finding]]
273	{apcs_finding}::
274	A classified region of
275	{apcs_plain_text}.
276	Produced by one of the three detection scans.
277	Each
278	{apcs_finding}
279	carries: the matched text, a byte offset and length in
280	{apcs_plain_text},
281	a
282	{apcs_severity},
283	a
284	{apcs_phi_category},
285	and a replacement string for anonymization.
286	
287	[[apcs_severity]]
288	{apcs_severity}::
289	The confidence level of a
290	{apcs_finding}.
291	Determines default elision behavior and visual presentation.
292	Three levels:
293	
294	[[apcs_red]]
295	{apcs_red}::
296	Definite PHI.
297	Produced by the
298	{apcs_regex_scan}
299	(structural match) or the
300	{apcs_label_scan}
301	(positional authority).
302	Default: elide.
303	
304	[[apcs_yellow]]
305	{apcs_yellow}::
306	Possible PHI.
307	Produced by the
308	{apcs_dictionary_scan}
309	when a word appears in a
310	{apcs_blacklist}.
311	Default: elide.
312	
313	[[apcs_pass]]
314	{apcs_pass}::
315	Not PHI.
316	A word appearing only in a
317	{apcs_whitelist}
318	and not in any
319	{apcs_blacklist}.
320	No
321	{apcs_finding}
322	is emitted for
323	{apcs_pass}
324	words.
325	
326	[[apcs_phi_category]]
327	{apcs_phi_category}::
328	The HIPAA Safe Harbor category of a
329	{apcs_finding}.
330	Determines the
331	{apcs_placeholder}
332	text used in
333	{apcs_anonymized_text}.
334	Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.
335	
336	
337	== Pipeline Steps
338	
339	The detection pipeline executes five steps in fixed order.
340	Each step either transforms input or produces
341	{apcs_finding_s}.
342	
343	[[apcs_parse]]
344	{apcs_parse}::
345	Transform
346	{apcs_epic_html}
347	into a
348	{apcs_document}:
349	a sequence of typed
350	{apcs_span_s}
351	and derived
352	{apcs_plain_text}.
353	HTML structure is consumed; output is position-mapped text.
354	
355	[[apcs_regex_scan]]
356	{apcs_regex_scan}::
357	Scan
358	{apcs_plain_text}
359	against every pattern in the
360	{apcs_regex_table}.
361	Each match emits a
362	{apcs_red}
363	{apcs_finding}
364	with the
365	{apcs_phi_category}
366	specified by the pattern.
367	
368	[[apcs_label_scan]]
369	{apcs_label_scan}::
370	For each
371	{apcs_labeled_field}
372	whose label appears in the
373	{apcs_label_registry}:
374	extract the value text, segment into words, and emit one
375	{apcs_red}
376	{apcs_finding}
377	per word with the
378	{apcs_phi_category}
379	specified by the registry entry.
380	
381	[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
382	{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
383	Scan
384	{apcs_plain_text}
385	against the combined
386	{apcs_blacklist}
387	automaton.
388	For each hit, check
389	{apcs_whitelist}
390	membership.
391	Emit a
392	{apcs_yellow}
393	{apcs_finding}
394	if the word is in any
395	{apcs_blacklist}
396	(regardless of
397	{apcs_whitelist}
398	membership).
399	Words in a
400	{apcs_whitelist}
401	only receive no
402	{apcs_finding}.
403	
404	[[apcs_merge]]
405	{apcs_merge}::
406	Combine all
407	{apcs_finding_s}
408	from the three scans.
409	When multiple
410	{apcs_finding_s}
411	occupy the same byte range, retain only the one with highest
412	{apcs_severity}
413	({apcs_red} > {apcs_yellow} > {apcs_pass}).
414	
415	
416	== Output Projections
417	
418	[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
419	{apcs_anonymized_text}::
420	{apcs_plain_text}
421	with elided
422	{apcs_finding_s}
423	replaced by their
424	{apcs_placeholder_s}.
425	Findings toggled to
426	{apcs_pass}
427	by the clinician pass through with original text.
428	The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.
429	
430	[[apcs_placeholder]]
431	{apcs_placeholder}::
432	A category-specific substitution string used in
433	{apcs_anonymized_text}.
434	Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
435	Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).
436	
437	[[apcs_assay]]
438	{apcs_assay}::
439	{apcs_plain_text}
440	with
441	{apcs_finding_s}
442	annotated inline using
443	{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
444	A diagnostic projection — shows the detection decisions within the document for review.
445	Not user-facing; used for pipeline quality assessment.
446	
447	[[apcs_elision_marker]]
448	{apcs_elision_marker}::
449	The annotation unit in an
450	{apcs_assay}.
451	Format: `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY matched text›` (guillemet-delimited).
452	Shows the
453	{apcs_severity},
454	{apcs_phi_category},
455	and original text of each
456	{apcs_finding}
457	inline with surrounding
458	{apcs_plain_text}.
459

{apcs_clipboard_content}

{apcs_clinical_gate}

== Parsed Structure

[[apcs_document]]

{apcs_epic_html}.

{apcs_span_s}
and the derived
{apcs_plain_text}.

[[apcs_plain_text]]
{apcs_plain_text}::
A single string derived from the
{apcs_document}
by stripping HTML markup, normalizing whitespace, and concatenating span contributions with structural line breaks.
All detection scans operate against
{apcs_plain_text}.
Offsets in
{apcs_finding_s}
are byte positions within this string.

[[apcs_span]]
{apcs_span}::
A typed region within the
{apcs_document}.
Each
{apcs_span}
has a kind, an optional label, text content, and a byte offset into
{apcs_plain_text}.
Three kinds:
{apcs_labeled_field},
{apcs_section_header},
{apcs_narrative}.

[[apcs_labeled_field]]
{apcs_labeled_field}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
The label is the bold text before the colon.
The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).

[[apcs_section_header]]
{apcs_section_header}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
Contributes the header text to
{apcs_plain_text}
but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
{apcs_span_s}.

[[apcs_narrative]]
{apcs_narrative}::
A
{apcs_span}
containing freetext not attached to a label.
Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
PHI in
{apcs_narrative}
{apcs_span_s}
lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
or
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.


== Detection Resources

=== Registries

[[apcs_label_registry]]
{apcs_label_registry}::
The enumerated set of
{apcs_labeled_field}
labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
{apcs_label_scan}.
Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
{apcs_phi_category}
assigned to extracted words.
+
[cols="2,1"]
|===
| Label | Category

| Patient:           | NAME
| Attending:         | PROVIDER
| Provider:          | PROVIDER
| Referring:         | PROVIDER
| Facility:          | FACILITY
| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
| Emergency Contact  | NAME
| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
|===

[[apcs_regex_table]]
{apcs_regex_table}::
The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Each entry specifies a pattern, a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a capture group index.
All matches produce
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding_s}.
See APCPS for the full pattern table.

=== Dictionaries

[[apcs_blacklist]]
{apcs_blacklist}::
A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Three named
{apcs_blacklist_s}
are combined into a single lookup automaton:

[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
{apcs_city_dictionary}::
US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_whitelist]]
{apcs_whitelist}::
A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
Membership in a
{apcs_whitelist}
does not prevent a
{apcs_finding} —
a word in both a
{apcs_blacklist}
and a
{apcs_whitelist}
is classified
{apcs_yellow}.
Two named
{apcs_whitelist_s}:

[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
{apcs_english_whitelist}::
Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
Source: original curation.


== Finding Anatomy

[[apcs_finding]]
{apcs_finding}::
A classified region of
{apcs_plain_text}.
Produced by one of the three detection scans.
Each
{apcs_finding}
carries: the matched text, a byte offset and length in
{apcs_plain_text},
a
{apcs_severity},
a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a replacement string for anonymization.

[[apcs_severity]]
{apcs_severity}::
The confidence level of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines default elision behavior and visual presentation.
Three levels:

[[apcs_red]]
{apcs_red}::
Definite PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
(structural match) or the
{apcs_label_scan}
(positional authority).
Default: elide.

[[apcs_yellow]]
{apcs_yellow}::
Possible PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}
when a word appears in a
{apcs_blacklist}.
Default: elide.

[[apcs_pass]]
{apcs_pass}::
Not PHI.
A word appearing only in a
{apcs_whitelist}
and not in any
{apcs_blacklist}.
No
{apcs_finding}
is emitted for
{apcs_pass}
words.

[[apcs_phi_category]]
{apcs_phi_category}::
The HIPAA Safe Harbor category of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines the
{apcs_placeholder}
text used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.


== Pipeline Steps

The detection pipeline executes five steps in fixed order.
Each step either transforms input or produces
{apcs_finding_s}.

[[apcs_parse]]
{apcs_parse}::
Transform
{apcs_epic_html}
into a
{apcs_document}:
a sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
and derived
{apcs_plain_text}.
HTML structure is consumed; output is position-mapped text.

[[apcs_regex_scan]]
{apcs_regex_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against every pattern in the
{apcs_regex_table}.
Each match emits a
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern.

[[apcs_label_scan]]
{apcs_label_scan}::
For each
{apcs_labeled_field}
whose label appears in the
{apcs_label_registry}:
extract the value text, segment into words, and emit one
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
per word with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry.

[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against the combined
{apcs_blacklist}
automaton.
For each hit, check
{apcs_whitelist}
membership.
Emit a
{apcs_yellow}
{apcs_finding}
if the word is in any
{apcs_blacklist}
(regardless of
{apcs_whitelist}
membership).
Words in a
{apcs_whitelist}
only receive no
{apcs_finding}.

[[apcs_merge]]
{apcs_merge}::
Combine all
{apcs_finding_s}
from the three scans.
When multiple
{apcs_finding_s}
occupy the same byte range, retain only the one with highest
{apcs_severity}
({apcs_red} > {apcs_yellow} > {apcs_pass}).


== Output Projections

[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
{apcs_anonymized_text}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with elided
{apcs_finding_s}
replaced by their
{apcs_placeholder_s}.
Findings toggled to
{apcs_pass}
by the clinician pass through with original text.
The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.

[[apcs_placeholder]]
{apcs_placeholder}::
A category-specific substitution string used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).

[[apcs_assay]]
{apcs_assay}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with
{apcs_finding_s}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
A diagnostic projection — shows the detection decisions within the document for review.
Not user-facing; used for pipeline quality assessment.

[[apcs_elision_marker]]
{apcs_elision_marker}::
The annotation unit in an
{apcs_assay}.
Format: `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY matched text›` (guillemet-delimited).
Shows the
{apcs_severity},
{apcs_phi_category},
and original text of each
{apcs_finding}
inline with surrounding
{apcs_plain_text}.

content.text:
## Character

Open design conversation requiring judgment. The question "how do multiple discerners' outputs get fused into a single unified classification?" has no mechanical answer — it requires choosing between competing shapes (role/disposition terminology, rule formulation, dictionary asymmetry handling, clinician-toggle composition). Opus-scale: several non-obvious decisions, each with downstream consequences through the spec and eventual code.

## Prerequisites

Pace ₢A9AAU (`apcs-discerner-restructure`) must land first. That pace installs the architectural skeleton (parallel discerners, normalized-text input, anonymization output) and stubs the reconciliation section. This pace fills the stub.

## Context

In the same session that produced ₢A9AAU, a second design conversation began but was deliberately deferred rather than rushed. The open questions are known and named; the answers are not.

## Open Questions Carried Forward

1. **Role / disposition terminology — what replaces severity?**
   - Candidate A: unified classification carries a role from `{TEXT, PHI, DECIDE}` per segment
   - Candidate B: findings carry a disposition from `{cover, surface}` individually
   - Candidate C: a third shape not yet surfaced
   - The choice has downstream effects on anonymization, triage UI, and every discerner output schema
2. **Dictionary internal disposition.** Does `apcs_dictionary_finding` carry a self-dispositioned field (confident vs. ambiguous, derived from whitelist corroboration), or does reconciliation derive this from external rules? Earlier conversation leaned toward internal — the dictionary has the whitelist, so it's the one with the evidence — but the asymmetry with other discerners needs to be documented honestly.
3. **Clinician toggle composition.** Three candidate shapes:
   - Clinician as a discerner producing `apcs_clinician_finding` that enters reconciliation
   - Clinician as a post-reconciliation mutator operating on `apcs_unified_classification`
   - Some third shape (e.g., clinician-as-disposition-override)
4. **Granularity reconciliation.** When two discerners produce findings at different granularities over overlapping text (e.g., `apcs_stanford_finding` covering `Margaret J. Thornton` and `apcs_dictionary_finding` covering just `Margaret`), how is the unified classification sized? Phrase wins? Union? Explicit rule per discerner pair?

## Scope

- Resolve each open question above with concrete design decisions
- Write the concrete reconciliation rules — pairwise as needed, following the "intimate, not generic" discipline. No `apcs_reliability_profile`; rules reference specific discerner outputs by name
- Update `apcs_reconciliation` in APCS0 from stub to authoritative
- Name any new quoins the rules require (candidates: `apcs_corroboration`, `apcs_subsumption`, `apcs_role`, `apcs_disposition`, `apcs_clinician_decision`)
- Cascade changes through APCS0 as decisions crystallize — finding outputs may gain fields; `apcs_unified_classification` receives its concrete shape

## Expected Concrete Rules (Starting Set, Open to Redirect)

These are hypotheses for the next session, not commitments:

- `apcs_regex_finding` is authoritative for its category — no corroboration needed, no contradiction admitted
- `apcs_stanford_finding(PATIENT)` overlapping a Patient-label `apcs_label_finding` → unified as the phrase-scoped finding (granularity merger)
- `apcs_dictionary_finding` uncorroborated by `apcs_stanford_finding` or an `apcs_label_finding` → surfaced for clinician decision
- `apcs_city_finding` from dictionary corroborated by `apcs_stanford_finding(HOSPITAL)` → unified as FACILITY (HOSPITAL subsumes CITY)
- Category authority table: per-category rule for which discerner wins when discerners disagree

## Non-goals

- No code changes to Rust sources (the spec drives code, not the other way around — code changes come in a later pace once APCS0 is authoritative)
- No changes to APCAS, APCPS, or `apck-claude-context.md` unless a cascade from APCS0 makes them incorrect
- No changes to the four discerners themselves — their outputs are the inputs to reconciliation, not the subject of this pace

## Exit Criteria

- `== Reconciliation` section in APCS0 is authoritative (no DEFERRED flags anywhere)
- Terminology for role/disposition is chosen, documented, and voiced in the right AXLA motifs
- Dictionary-finding schema is decided and expressed in the `apcs_dictionary_finding` quoin
- Clinician-toggle composition is decided and expressed as a quoin or as a rule
- Every rule references specific discerner outputs by name — no generic abstractions
- A reader can trace the complete path from `apcs_normalized_text` through each discerner, through reconciliation, to `apcs_unified_classification`, and on to anonymized output — no gaps, no hand-waving
- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` still passes (the spec change is not code, but no cascade should break tests)

== Overview

This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
It specifies the entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the procedures it performs.

The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
This document owns the detection pipeline's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.

The pipeline has three named procedures with four parallel discerners between the first and second.
{apcs_normalization}
produces the canonical input.
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_label_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
and
{apcs_stanford_ner}
each run as a pure function of
{apcs_normalized_text},
producing their own intimately-named finding type.
{apcs_reconciliation}
fuses the four finding streams into
{apcs_unified_classification}.
{apcs_anonymization}
produces
{apcs_anonymized_text}.

Byte offsets, character arithmetic, and tokenizer mechanics live in code.
This vocabulary talks about regions of
{apcs_normalized_text} —
the addressable units of detection — and leaves their concrete representation as an implementation concern.


== Input

[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
{apcs_clipboard_content}::
Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
May be HTML or plain text.
Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.

[[apcs_epic_html]]
{apcs_epic_html}::
HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.

[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
{apcs_clinical_gate}::
A heuristic that decides whether
{apcs_clipboard_content}
is clinical.
Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
Content that fails the
{apcs_clinical_gate}
is not processed; the triage view clears.


== Parsed Structure

The parsed structure captures the semantic regions Epic's HTML encodes — labeled demographic fields, section headers, narrative prose.
Parsing is an implementation-level step of
{apcs_normalization};
this vocabulary exists because the narrative discussion of individual discerners refers to structural categories when explaining how they decide.
Region membership belongs to the concept model; byte offsets and string arithmetic do not.

[[apcs_document]]
{apcs_document}::
The result of parsing
{apcs_epic_html}.
A sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
whose concatenated text contributions form
{apcs_normalized_text}.

[[apcs_span]]
{apcs_span}::
A typed region within the
{apcs_document}.
Each
{apcs_span}
has a kind, an optional label, and text content.
Three kinds:
{apcs_labeled_field},
{apcs_section_header},
{apcs_narrative}.

[[apcs_labeled_field]]
{apcs_labeled_field}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
The label is the bold text before the colon.
The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).

[[apcs_section_header]]
{apcs_section_header}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
Contributes the header text to
{apcs_normalized_text}
but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
{apcs_span_s}.

[[apcs_narrative]]
{apcs_narrative}::
A
{apcs_span}
containing freetext not attached to a label.
Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
PHI in
{apcs_narrative}
{apcs_span_s}
lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
or
{apcs_stanford_ner}.


== Detection Resources

=== Registries

[[apcs_label_registry]]
{apcs_label_registry}::
The enumerated set of
{apcs_labeled_field}
labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
{apcs_label_scan}.
Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
{apcs_phi_category}
assigned to extracted words.
+
[cols="2,1"]
|===
| Label | Category

| Patient:           | NAME
| Attending:         | PROVIDER
| Provider:          | PROVIDER
| Referring:         | PROVIDER
| Facility:          | FACILITY
| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
| Emergency Contact  | NAME
| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
|===

[[apcs_regex_table]]
{apcs_regex_table}::
The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Each entry specifies a pattern, a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a capture group index.
See APCPS for the full pattern table.

=== Dictionaries

[[apcs_blacklist]]
{apcs_blacklist}::
A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Three named
{apcs_blacklist_s}
are combined into a single lookup automaton:

[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
{apcs_city_dictionary}::
US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_whitelist]]
{apcs_whitelist}::
A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
A
{apcs_whitelist}
supplies corroborating evidence about a word — specifically, that the word has a recognized non-PHI reading.
The
{apcs_dictionary_scan}
records whitelist membership as a flag on every emitted
{apcs_dictionary_finding}
but does not use it to gate emission; how whitelist corroboration composes into a disposition is a
{apcs_reconciliation}
concern.
Two named
{apcs_whitelist_s}:

[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
{apcs_english_whitelist}::
Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
Source: original curation.


=== PHI Categories

[[apcs_phi_category]]
{apcs_phi_category}::
The HIPAA Safe Harbor category attached to findings that identify PHI.
Determines the
{apcs_placeholder}
text used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.
The
{apcs_stanford_ner}
discerner carries its own flat taxonomy of native labels (PATIENT, DOCTOR, HOSPITAL, …);
{apcs_reconciliation}
is responsible for mapping those labels into the
{apcs_phi_category}
enumeration.


== Normalization

[[apcs_normalization]]
{apcs_normalization}::
The one-time light-cleanup procedure that produces the canonical input for every discerner.
Consumes
{apcs_clipboard_content} —
parsed as
{apcs_epic_html}
into a
{apcs_document}
when the content is HTML, or taken as-is when plain text — and produces
{apcs_normalized_text}.
One of the three named procedures of the pipeline, together with
{apcs_reconciliation}
and
{apcs_anonymization}.
Internal mechanics (HTML stripping, whitespace collapse, line-break handling, encoding normalization) are implementation detail; the vocabulary concerns only the procedure's inputs and output.

[[apcs_normalized_text]]
{apcs_normalized_text}::
The canonical string every discerner consumes.
The output of
{apcs_normalization}.
Regions within
{apcs_normalized_text}
are the addressable units of detection; byte positions and character offsets are implementation detail and not part of this vocabulary.
All four discerners —
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_label_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
{apcs_stanford_ner} —
are pure functions of
{apcs_normalized_text}.


== Discerners

Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
{apcs_normalized_text}
and emits an intimately-named finding type.
The four discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
{apcs_reconciliation}
fuses their finding streams.

There is deliberately no generic supertype for discerners and no generic finding type.
Each discerner is named individually, parameterized individually, and reasoned about individually;
{apcs_reconciliation}
rules reference discerners and findings by name, not by a shared abstract shape.
This is load-bearing: earlier drafts that introduced `apcs_algorithm` and `apcs_reliability_profile` collapsed distinct concerns (what a discerner knows, how confident it is, how its output should be displayed) into non-load-bearing abstractions that each had to be re-specialized at every rule site.

=== Regex Scan

[[apcs_regex_scan]]
{apcs_regex_scan}::
The discerner that detects structurally identifiable PHI by regular-expression pattern matching.
Parameterized by the
{apcs_regex_table}.
Scans
{apcs_normalized_text}
against every pattern.
Each match emits one
{apcs_regex_finding}
with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern entry.

[[apcs_regex_finding]]
{apcs_regex_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, and the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern that fired.

=== Label Scan

[[apcs_label_scan]]
{apcs_label_scan}::
The discerner that extracts PHI positionally from recognized Epic labels.
Parameterized by the
{apcs_label_registry}.
For each registry entry whose label text appears in
{apcs_normalized_text},
segment the value following the label into words and emit one
{apcs_label_finding}
per word with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry.

[[apcs_label_finding]]
{apcs_label_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_label_scan}.
Carries: the word text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry, and the originating label (retained for audit and for reconciliation rules that reference specific labels).

=== Dictionary Scan

[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
The discerner that detects potential PHI by dictionary membership.
Parameterized by the combined
{apcs_blacklist}
automaton and the two
{apcs_whitelist_s}.
Scans
{apcs_normalized_text}
against the blacklist automaton; every hit emits one
{apcs_dictionary_finding},
regardless of whitelist membership.
Whitelist corroboration is recorded on the emitted finding as a flag — it is evidence passed downstream, not a gate on emission.
+
NOTE: The internal disposition of
{apcs_dictionary_finding_s}
(how the whitelist-collision flag composes with the blacklist hit to express self-confidence, and whether the dictionary itself renders an ambiguous-vs-confident verdict or defers that entirely to reconciliation) is a deferred decision of
{apcs_reconciliation} —
see ₢A9AAV `apcs-combining-algorithm`.

[[apcs_dictionary_finding]]
{apcs_dictionary_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the originating
{apcs_blacklist}
(surname / firstname / city), and a whitelist-collision flag indicating whether the word is also in an
{apcs_english_whitelist}
or
{apcs_medical_whitelist}.
A
{apcs_phi_category}
is implied by the originating dictionary (NAME for surname/firstname, ADDRESS for city), subject to any remapping in
{apcs_reconciliation}.

=== Stanford NER

[[apcs_stanford_ner]]
{apcs_stanford_ner}::
The discerner that detects PHI by neural named-entity recognition.
Parameterized by a frozen ONNX export of `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` together with its flat taxonomy of entity labels (PATIENT, DOCTOR, HOSPITAL, DATE, AGE, STREET, CITY, STATE, COUNTRY, ZIP, PHONE, EMAIL, ID, PROFESSION, ORGANIZATION, …).
Runs as a pure function of
{apcs_normalized_text}:
tokenizes the input, scores each token, and emits a
{apcs_stanford_finding}
per contiguous labeled span.
The taxonomy is the model's native label set;
{apcs_reconciliation}
maps native labels onto the
{apcs_phi_category}
enumeration when building
{apcs_unified_classification}.

[[apcs_stanford_finding]]
{apcs_stanford_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_stanford_ner}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the native Stanford entity label, and the model's confidence score for that span.


== Reconciliation

NOTE: **Deferred section.**
The reconciliation algorithm — the rules that fuse the four discerner finding streams into a single authoritative classification — is the subject of pace ₢A9AAV `apcs-combining-algorithm`.
The quoins below are placeholders so downstream procedures have referenceable inputs and so the document's link graph remains complete; their authoritative shape is written as part of that pace.

Specifically deferred to ₢A9AAV:

* The role / disposition vocabulary that replaces the discarded severity gestalt (candidates under discussion include TEXT/PHI/DECIDE as classification roles, `cover`/`surface` as per-finding dispositions, and others).
* Per-discerner-pair precedence rules, including granularity merging when findings of different sizes overlap (e.g., a phrase-scoped
{apcs_stanford_finding}
covering a word-scoped
{apcs_label_finding}).
* The composition of dictionary-finding internal disposition with reconciliation rules (whether the dictionary renders a self-verdict using its whitelist evidence, or whether reconciliation derives the verdict externally).
* Clinician-toggle composition — whether clinician decisions enter reconciliation as a fifth finding stream, operate as a post-reconciliation mutator on
{apcs_unified_classification},
or take some third shape.
* The concrete mapping from
{apcs_stanford_ner}
native labels onto the
{apcs_phi_category}
enumeration.

[[apcs_reconciliation]]
{apcs_reconciliation}::
The procedure that fuses the
{apcs_regex_finding_s},
{apcs_label_finding_s},
{apcs_dictionary_finding_s},
and
{apcs_stanford_finding_s}
covering regions of
{apcs_normalized_text}
into
{apcs_unified_classification}.
One of the three named procedures of the pipeline, together with
{apcs_normalization}
and
{apcs_anonymization}.
Rules — including all items listed above — are deferred to ₢A9AAV.

[[apcs_unified_classification]]
{apcs_unified_classification}::
The output of
{apcs_reconciliation}:
a single authoritative classification of every covered region of
{apcs_normalized_text},
consumed by
{apcs_anonymization}.
The concrete shape of a classification (the fields carried, the role/disposition vocabulary, the clinician-toggle composition) is deferred to ₢A9AAV.


== Anonymization

[[apcs_anonymization]]
{apcs_anonymization}::
The procedure that consumes
{apcs_unified_classification}
and
{apcs_normalized_text}
and produces
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Regions classified as PHI are replaced by their category-specific
{apcs_placeholder_s};
regions classified as retained text pass through verbatim.
One of the three named procedures of the pipeline, named for symmetry with
{apcs_normalization}
and
{apcs_reconciliation}.

[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
{apcs_anonymized_text}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every region marked for elision in
{apcs_unified_classification}
replaced by its
{apcs_placeholder}.
The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.

[[apcs_placeholder]]
{apcs_placeholder}::
A category-specific substitution string emitted by
{apcs_anonymization}.
Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).


== Diagnostic Projections

The assay projections render the pipeline's internal state for review and debugging.
They are not user-facing — the user-facing surface is
{apcs_anonymized_text}
together with the triage view (owned by APCAS).
Two sibling projections exist because the pipeline now has two meaningful stages to inspect: the raw per-discerner output before
{apcs_reconciliation},
and the fused view after.

[[apcs_discerner_assay]]
{apcs_discerner_assay}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every
{apcs_regex_finding},
{apcs_label_finding},
{apcs_dictionary_finding},
and
{apcs_stanford_finding}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
Shows each discerner's raw output — overlaps, disagreements, and granularity differences are all visible side-by-side, which is the point.

[[apcs_unified_assay]]
{apcs_unified_assay}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every region of
{apcs_unified_classification}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
Shows the post-reconciliation view — what
{apcs_anonymization}
actually sees.

[[apcs_elision_marker]]
{apcs_elision_marker}::
The annotation unit in an
{apcs_discerner_assay}
or an
{apcs_unified_assay}.
In an
{apcs_discerner_assay}
the format is `‹DISCERNER:CATEGORY matched text›` (e.g., `‹REGEX:SSN 123-45-6789›`, `‹DICTIONARY:NAME Margaret›`, `‹STANFORD:HOSPITAL Mercy General›`) so each discerner's contribution is distinguishable.
In an
{apcs_unified_assay}
the format is `‹CATEGORY matched text›` — the discerner that originated the region is absorbed into the reconciliation decision and no longer shown.
Guillemets delimit the marker to avoid collision with clinical prose punctuation.

== Overview

This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
It specifies the entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the procedures it performs.

The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
This document owns the detection pipeline's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.

The pipeline has three named procedures with four parallel discerners between the first and second.
{apcs_normalization}
produces the canonical input.
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_label_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
and
{apcs_stanford_ner}
each run as a pure function of
{apcs_normalized_text},
producing their own intimately-named finding type.
{apcs_reconciliation}
fuses the four finding streams into
{apcs_unified_classification}.
{apcs_anonymization}
produces
{apcs_anonymized_text}.

Byte offsets, character arithmetic, and tokenizer mechanics live in code.
This vocabulary talks about regions of
{apcs_normalized_text} —
the addressable units of detection — and leaves their concrete representation as an implementation concern.


== Input

[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
{apcs_clipboard_content}::
Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
May be HTML or plain text.
Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.

[[apcs_epic_html]]
{apcs_epic_html}::
HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.

[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
{apcs_clinical_gate}::
A heuristic that decides whether
{apcs_clipboard_content}
is clinical.
Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
Content that fails the
{apcs_clinical_gate}
is not processed; the triage view clears.


== Parsed Structure

The parsed structure captures the semantic regions Epic's HTML encodes — labeled demographic fields, section headers, narrative prose.
Parsing is an implementation-level step of
{apcs_normalization};
this vocabulary exists because the narrative discussion of individual discerners refers to structural categories when explaining how they decide.
Region membership belongs to the concept model; byte offsets and string arithmetic do not.

[[apcs_document]]
{apcs_document}::
The result of parsing
{apcs_epic_html}.
A sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
whose concatenated text contributions form
{apcs_normalized_text}.

[[apcs_span]]
{apcs_span}::
A typed region within the
{apcs_document}.
Each
{apcs_span}
has a kind, an optional label, and text content.
Three kinds:
{apcs_labeled_field},
{apcs_section_header},
{apcs_narrative}.

[[apcs_labeled_field]]
{apcs_labeled_field}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
The label is the bold text before the colon.
The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).

[[apcs_section_header]]
{apcs_section_header}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
Contributes the header text to
{apcs_normalized_text}
but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
{apcs_span_s}.

[[apcs_narrative]]
{apcs_narrative}::
A
{apcs_span}
containing freetext not attached to a label.
Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
PHI in
{apcs_narrative}
{apcs_span_s}
lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
or
{apcs_stanford_ner}.

[[apcs_normalized_text]]
{apcs_normalized_text}::
The canonical string every discerner consumes.
The output of
{apcs_normalization}.
Regions within
{apcs_normalized_text}
are the addressable units of detection; byte positions and character offsets are implementation detail and not part of this vocabulary.
All four discerners —
{apcs_regex_scan},
{apcs_label_scan},
{apcs_dictionary_scan},
{apcs_stanford_ner} —
are pure functions of
{apcs_normalized_text}.


== Discerners

Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
{apcs_normalized_text}
and emits an intimately-named finding type.
The four discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
{apcs_reconciliation}
fuses their finding streams.

There is deliberately no generic supertype for discerners and no generic finding type.
Each discerner is named individually, parameterized individually, and reasoned about individually;
{apcs_reconciliation}
rules reference discerners and findings by name, not by a shared abstract shape.
This is load-bearing: earlier drafts that introduced `apcs_algorithm` and `apcs_reliability_profile` collapsed distinct concerns (what a discerner knows, how confident it is, how its output should be displayed) into non-load-bearing abstractions that each had to be re-specialized at every rule site.

=== Regex Scan

[[apcs_regex_scan]]
{apcs_regex_scan}::
The discerner that detects structurally identifiable PHI by regular-expression pattern matching.
Parameterized by the
{apcs_regex_table}.
Scans
{apcs_normalized_text}
against every pattern.
Each match emits one
{apcs_regex_finding}
with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern entry.

[[apcs_regex_finding]]
{apcs_regex_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, and the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern that fired.

=== Label Scan

[[apcs_label_scan]]
{apcs_label_scan}::
The discerner that extracts PHI positionally from recognized Epic labels.
Parameterized by the
{apcs_label_registry}.
For each registry entry whose label text appears in
{apcs_normalized_text},
segment the value following the label into words and emit one
{apcs_label_finding}
per word with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry.

[[apcs_label_finding]]
{apcs_label_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_label_scan}.
Carries: the word text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry, and the originating label (retained for audit and for reconciliation rules that reference specific labels).

=== Dictionary Scan

[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
The discerner that detects potential PHI by dictionary membership.
Parameterized by the combined
{apcs_blacklist}
automaton and the two
{apcs_whitelist_s}.
Scans
{apcs_normalized_text}
against the blacklist automaton; every hit emits one
{apcs_dictionary_finding},
regardless of whitelist membership.
Whitelist corroboration is recorded on the emitted finding as a flag — it is evidence passed downstream, not a gate on emission.
+
NOTE: The internal disposition of
{apcs_dictionary_finding_s}
(how the whitelist-collision flag composes with the blacklist hit to express self-confidence, and whether the dictionary itself renders an ambiguous-vs-confident verdict or defers that entirely to reconciliation) is a deferred decision of
{apcs_reconciliation} —
see ₢A9AAV `apcs-combining-algorithm`.

[[apcs_dictionary_finding]]
{apcs_dictionary_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the originating
{apcs_blacklist}
(surname / firstname / city), and a whitelist-collision flag indicating whether the word is also in an
{apcs_english_whitelist}
or
{apcs_medical_whitelist}.
A
{apcs_phi_category}
is implied by the originating dictionary (NAME for surname/firstname, ADDRESS for city), subject to any remapping in
{apcs_reconciliation}.

=== Stanford NER

[[apcs_stanford_ner]]
{apcs_stanford_ner}::
The discerner that detects PHI by neural named-entity recognition.
Parameterized by a frozen ONNX export of `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` together with its flat taxonomy of entity labels (PATIENT, DOCTOR, HOSPITAL, DATE, AGE, STREET, CITY, STATE, COUNTRY, ZIP, PHONE, EMAIL, ID, PROFESSION, ORGANIZATION, …).
Runs as a pure function of
{apcs_normalized_text}:
tokenizes the input, scores each token, and emits a
{apcs_stanford_finding}
per contiguous labeled span.
The taxonomy is the model's native label set;
{apcs_reconciliation}
maps native labels onto the
{apcs_phi_category}
enumeration when building
{apcs_unified_classification}.

[[apcs_stanford_finding]]
{apcs_stanford_finding}::
The output unit of
{apcs_stanford_ner}.
Carries: the matched text, the region of
{apcs_normalized_text}
it covers, the native Stanford entity label, and the model's confidence score for that span.


== Reconciliation

NOTE: **Deferred section.**
The reconciliation algorithm — the rules that fuse the four discerner finding streams into a single authoritative classification — is the subject of pace ₢A9AAV `apcs-combining-algorithm`.
The quoins below are placeholders so downstream procedures have referenceable inputs and so the document's link graph remains complete; their authoritative shape is written as part of that pace.

Specifically deferred to ₢A9AAV:

* The role / disposition vocabulary that replaces the discarded severity gestalt (candidates under discussion include TEXT/PHI/DECIDE as classification roles, `cover`/`surface` as per-finding dispositions, and others).
* Per-discerner-pair precedence rules, including granularity merging when findings of different sizes overlap (e.g., a phrase-scoped
{apcs_stanford_finding}
covering a word-scoped
{apcs_label_finding}).
* The composition of dictionary-finding internal disposition with reconciliation rules (whether the dictionary renders a self-verdict using its whitelist evidence, or whether reconciliation derives the verdict externally).
* Clinician-toggle composition — whether clinician decisions enter reconciliation as a fifth finding stream, operate as a post-reconciliation mutator on
{apcs_unified_classification},
or take some third shape.
* The concrete mapping from
{apcs_stanford_ner}
native labels onto the
{apcs_phi_category}
enumeration.

[[apcs_reconciliation]]
{apcs_reconciliation}::
The procedure that fuses the
{apcs_regex_finding_s},
{apcs_label_finding_s},
{apcs_dictionary_finding_s},
and
{apcs_stanford_finding_s}
covering regions of
{apcs_normalized_text}
into
{apcs_unified_classification}.
One of the three named procedures of the pipeline, together with
{apcs_normalization}
and
{apcs_anonymization}.
Rules — including all items listed above — are deferred to ₢A9AAV.

[[apcs_unified_classification]]
{apcs_unified_classification}::
The output of
{apcs_reconciliation}:
a single authoritative classification of every covered region of
{apcs_normalized_text},
consumed by
{apcs_anonymization}.
The concrete shape of a classification (the fields carried, the role/disposition vocabulary, the clinician-toggle composition) is deferred to ₢A9AAV.


== Anonymization

[[apcs_anonymization]]
{apcs_anonymization}::
The procedure that consumes
{apcs_unified_classification}
and
{apcs_normalized_text}
and produces
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Regions classified as PHI are replaced by their category-specific
{apcs_placeholder_s};
regions classified as retained text pass through verbatim.
One of the three named procedures of the pipeline, named for symmetry with
{apcs_normalization}
and
{apcs_reconciliation}.

[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
{apcs_anonymized_text}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every region marked for elision in
{apcs_unified_classification}
replaced by its
{apcs_placeholder}.
The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.

[[apcs_placeholder]]
{apcs_placeholder}::
A category-specific substitution string emitted by
{apcs_anonymization}.
Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).


== Diagnostic Projections

The assay projections render the pipeline's internal state for review and debugging.
They are not user-facing — the user-facing surface is
{apcs_anonymized_text}
together with the triage view (owned by APCAS).
Two sibling projections exist because the pipeline now has two meaningful stages to inspect: the raw per-discerner output before
{apcs_reconciliation},
and the fused view after.

[[apcs_discerner_assay]]
{apcs_discerner_assay}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every
{apcs_regex_finding},
{apcs_label_finding},
{apcs_dictionary_finding},
and
{apcs_stanford_finding}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
Shows each discerner's raw output — overlaps, disagreements, and granularity differences are all visible side-by-side, which is the point.

[[apcs_unified_assay]]
{apcs_unified_assay}::
{apcs_normalized_text}
with every region of
{apcs_unified_classification}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
Shows the post-reconciliation view — what
{apcs_anonymization}
actually sees.

[[apcs_elision_marker]]
{apcs_elision_marker}::
The annotation unit in an
{apcs_discerner_assay}
or an
{apcs_unified_assay}.
In an
{apcs_discerner_assay}
the format is `‹DISCERNER:CATEGORY matched text›` (e.g., `‹REGEX:SSN 123-45-6789›`, `‹DICTIONARY:NAME Margaret›`, `‹STANFORD:HOSPITAL Mercy General›`) so each discerner's contribution is distinguishable.
In an
{apcs_unified_assay}
the format is `‹CATEGORY matched text›` — the discerner that originated the region is absorbed into the reconciliation decision and no longer shown.
Guillemets delimit the marker to avoid collision with clinical prose punctuation.

|---|---|

New prose: "every hit emits … regardless of whitelist membership. Whitelist corroboration is recorded on the emitted finding as a flag — evidence passed downstream, not a gate on emission." |
| AsciiDoc renders without errors | Couldn't run `asciidoctor` (not installed). Structural checks clean: balanced brackets, correct `+` NOTE continuation, 36:36 anchor/def balance |

**New structure:**
- Overview (rewritten — names the pipeline shape)
- Input (unchanged)
- Parsed Structure (framed as intermediate to `apcs_normalization`; byte-offset language removed)
- Detection Resources (PHI Categories subsection added; Finding Anatomy section removed)

**Caveats worth flagging:**

1. **Rename not explicit in docket.** I treated `apcs_plain_text` → `apcs_normalized_text` as a rename (the old concept and the new one describe the same thing). The docket lists `apcs_normalized_text` under "Quoins to install" but doesn't list `apcs_plain_text` under "Quoins to remove." If you wanted them as two distinct concepts (e.g., plain_text = post-parse, normalized_text = post-cleanup), the edit would need splitting.
2. **`apcs_parse` removed as vocabulary.** The docket's "three procedures of the pipeline" (normalization/reconciliation/anonymization) excludes parse. I made parsing implementation-internal to normalization and said so explicitly in the Parsed Structure section preamble. If parse should remain a named procedure, we'd need a fourth procedure quoin.
3. **AsciiDoc render not locally verified.** Structural checks are green but only a real `asciidoctor` run will catch every edge case. If you have a render target elsewhere I can invoke, say the word.
